title: Prior Period Adjustment
event: In 2026, Delgado Co. discovers that 2025 depreciation was understated by $30,000 (ignore taxes). Prepare the correcting entry.

entries.label: Fix the past through RE, not this year's income

lines.label: prior year's error absorbed
lines.why: Last year's overstated income already closed into RE — so RE is where the fix lands.
lines.trap: NOT Depreciation Expense. Running it through 2026 income punishes the wrong year twice.

lines.label: catch-up
lines.why: The contra-asset catches up to where it should have been.
lines.trap: The asset's cost is untouched — only the accumulated depreciation was short.

memorize.body: Error corrections restate: adjust beginning RE (net of tax, when taxes apply) and fix the balance-sheet account. Current income is untouched.
On the retained earnings statement it appears as an adjustment to the BEGINNING balance — labeled 'prior period adjustment.'

questions.prompt: The $30,000 prior-year depreciation error is corrected in 2026. Effect on 2026 NET INCOME = ?

distractors.feedback: You expensed it in 2026 — but the cost belongs to 2025. Corrections route through beginning Retained Earnings, not current income.

build.scaffold: Ask which year's income was wrong. If it's a CLOSED year, the debit goes to RE.
